paper_title: Lipid Extraction and Sediment Core Analysis

methods.description: Process for extracting and analyzing lipids from sediment samples to study GDGTs over the deglaciation-Holocene transition.

steps.description: A total of 102 freeze-dried samples at 4 cm interval were collected for GDGT analysis over the last deglaciation-Holocene transition.
steps.searchable_summary: collect freeze-dried sediment samples for GDGT analysis

steps.description: A ∼ 4 g aliquot of each sample was extracted ultrasonically (4 times) with a mixture of dichloromethane and methanol (9 : 1, v/v).
steps.searchable_summary: ultrasonically extract lipids using dichloromethane and methanol

steps.description: The supernatants were condensed and saponified at room temperature for 12 h with a 1 M KOH/methanol solution.
steps.searchable_summary: condense and saponify supernatants with KOH/methanol

steps.description: The neutral fractions were then separated into apolar and polar fractions on a silica gel column, using n-hexane and methanol, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: separate neutral fractions using silica gel column

steps.description: The polar fraction containing the GDGTs was concentrated and filtered through 0.45 µm polytetrafluoroethylene syringe filters using n-hexane/isopropanol (99 : 1 v/v) and then dried under N2.
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate and filter polar fraction, then dry under N2

steps.description: GDGTs were analysed using an Agilent 1260 series HPLC-APCI-MS, following the procedure of Yang et al. (2015).

methods.description: Procedure for retrieving sediment core samples and establishing a chronological framework using radiocarbon dating.

steps.description: In summer 2016, an 874 cm long sediment core (CH2016) was retrieved using a UWITEC coring platform system with a percussion corer in 30 m of water depth.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve sediment core using UWITEC coring platform

steps.description: Each section of the core was split lengthways, photographed and then sectioned at a 1 cm interval in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: split, photograph, and section sediment core

steps.description: The chronology was established using AMS 14C dating of eight terrestrial plant macrofossils and charcoal.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS 14C dating on plant macrofossils and charcoal

steps.description: The age model was developed utilising Bacon, implemented in R 3.1.0 at 5 cm intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: develop age model using Bacon in R

steps.description: All AMS 14C dates were calibrated to calendar years before present using the program Calib 7.1 and the IntCal13 calibration data set.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate AMS 14C dates using Calib 7.1 and IntCal13
